title: lathyrus latifolius subsp. algericus
intro: Lathyrus latifolius subsp. algericus, also known as the Algerian pea or broad-leaved vetchling, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of isoleucine, a unique amino acid. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including the potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and improve cognitive function. In herbal preparations, Lathyrus latifolius subsp. algericus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
